title: galium aureum subsp. umbellulatum
intro: Galium aureum subsp. umbellulatum, also known as small bedstraw, is a subspecies of the galium aureum plant, a member of the bedstraw family (Rubiaceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving urinary tract issues, and acting as an antiseptic. The herbal preparations of Galium aureum subsp. umbellulatum typically involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves and stems, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
